feed.summary: Macerich Co. (MAC) released its 2025 10-K report, highlighting an 8.7% increase in Funds From Operations (FFO) to $396.9 million and the implementation of its "Path Forward Plan" to deleverage its capital structure. The REIT reported a net loss of $197.1 million due to interest and depreciation expenses but maintained a strong 94.0% leased occupancy rate across its 39 million square foot retail portfolio.

feed.title: Personal loans surge amid affordability struggles: It's 'the middle-class refinancing option,' expert says
feed.summary: Personal loan originations are predicted to be the primary driver of consumer credit growth in 2026, according to TransUnion, outpacing mortgages, credit cards, and auto loans. This surge is largely attributed to personal loans becoming a "middle-class refinancing option" for high-interest credit card debt. Subprime borrowers, those with lower credit scores, are significantly fueling this growth as they seek to consolidate debt, though experts caution that interest rate relief for them may be limited.

feed.title: Seven Hills Realty Trust (NASDAQ:SEVN) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Seven Hills Realty Trust announced strong Q4 2025 results, reporting distributable earnings of $0.28 per share, aided by successfully completing a $61.5 million rights offering which increased its investment capacity. The company expanded its loan portfolio to $724.5 million across 24 first mortgage loans and maintains a fully performing portfolio with no past due loans. Management projects first-quarter distributable earnings to be between $0.22 and $0.24 per share, anticipating portfolio growth to approximately $1 billion by year-end 2026, driven by new originations and redeployment of capital from loan repayments.

feed.title: BlackRock Signals $270M Bitcoin, Ethereum Sell-Off as $2.4B in Crypto Options Expire
feed.summary: BlackRock has deposited $270 million worth of Bitcoin and Ethereum into Coinbase, indicating a potential sell-off amidst significant cryptocurrency ETF outflows. This move coincides with the expiration of $2.4 billion in crypto options, which could lead to increased market volatility. Institutions are reportedly becoming more cautious about crypto investments due to quantum computing risks, contributing to the ongoing outflows from crypto ETFs.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries, Inc. $MHK Shares Sold by JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. reduced its stake in Mohawk Industries, Inc. (NYSE:MHK) by 27.6% during the third quarter, selling 968,414 shares. Despite this, JPMorgan Chase & Co. still owned 4.11% of the company's stock, valued at over $327 million. Other hedge funds, including Yousif Capital Management LLC and Resona Asset Management Co. Ltd., increased their holdings, while insiders have sold over 11,985 shares in the past 90 days.

feed.title: NEOS Investment Management LLC Purchases 19,502 Shares of Citizens Financial Group, Inc. $CFG
feed.summary: NEOS Investment Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) by 46.8%, acquiring an additional 19,502 shares to bring its total holdings to 61,147 shares valued at $3.25 million. This move highlights institutional confidence in CFG, which reported strong third-quarter earnings of $1.13 EPS, exceeding estimates, and revenue growth of 8.6% year-over-year. The bank also recently distributed a quarterly dividend of $0.46 and holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from Wall Street analysts.

feed.title: Eurozone upturn buoyed in February as flash manufacturing PMI hits 44-month high
feed.summary: The Eurozone experienced an improved economic growth in February, driven by a strong manufacturing sector which saw its PMI reach a 44-month high. While the service sector's momentum slowed slightly, the overall combined data suggests a quarterly GDP growth of 0.2% for the first quarter of 2026. Price pressures remained largely contained, though rising wage costs contributed to a stall in hiring across the eurozone.

feed.title: Liberty Wealth Buys $19 Million of Dimensional Global Ex US Core Fixed Income ETF
feed.summary: Liberty Wealth Management LLC recently acquired 367,041 shares of Dimensional Global Ex US Core Fixed Income ETF (DFGX) for an estimated value of $19.3 million, representing a new position of 2.5% of their reported U.S. equity assets. This investment reflects a balanced strategy to pursue growth and maintain income, leveraging DFGX for diversified exposure to global ex-US fixed-income markets, particularly in anticipation of further rate cuts. The ETF offers a 2.82% annualized dividend yield and provides stability, complementing growth-oriented assets in their portfolio.

feed.title: Apollo Global’s $5.9 Billion Earnings in 2025: What the Numbers Really Mean
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management reported record 2025 results with $5.9 billion in combined fee and spread-related earnings, driven by a record $309 billion in asset origination. Despite legal scrutiny regarding historical executive ties and regulatory pressure on private credit, the firm reached $938 billion in AUM and projected significant growth for 2026.

feed.title: Valley Bank’s Middle-Market Survey Highlights Strong Performance in a Shifting Environment
feed.summary: Valley National Bank's second annual Middle-Market C&I Survey indicates strong financial performance for middle-market businesses in 2025, with significant gains in cash flow, productivity, and profitability. However, these businesses anticipate increased external pressures in 2026, such as inflation, interest rates, and geopolitical tensions. The survey also highlights execution gaps in critical areas like cash flow management, fraud protection, and leveraging banker advisory relationships.

feed.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Rise Ahead Of Q4 GDP Numbers—Grail, Candel Therapeutics, Copart In Focus
feed.summary: U.S. stock futures are rising ahead of Q4 GDP data and the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge. Key companies like Copart, Candel Therapeutics, and Grail are in focus due to earnings announcements, stock offerings, and price trends. Analysts like Scott Wren of Wells Fargo maintain a bullish outlook on the U.S. economy, recommending investment in growth-sensitive sectors like Financials and Industrials.

feed.title: Regional Management Corp: Quiet Stock, Big Credit Risk Question for 2025
feed.summary: Regional Management Corp (NYSE: RM) is currently a high-sensitivity play on US subprime loan performance and Federal Reserve policy. The small-cap lender faces potential volatility tied to consumer credit stress and labor market strength, offering a high earnings yield for investors willing to underwrite non-prime household risk.

feed.title: Regional Management Corp: Quiet Stock, Big Credit Risk Question for 2025
feed.summary: Regional Management Corp (RM) is a subprime installment lender whose stock is directly tied to US consumer credit health and Federal Reserve policy. Investors need to closely monitor its credit trends like net charge-offs and delinquencies, as well as funding costs. While it offers a high earnings yield for those willing to underwrite non-prime US household risk, its sensitivity to credit quality means rapid movement when sentiment shifts, making it a leveraged bet on the US labor market and interest rate environment.

feed.title: Duke Energy’s Quiet Re?Rating: Defensive Stock or Value Trap for 2025?
feed.summary: Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK), one of the largest regulated utilities in the US, is highlighted as a defensive play offering a steady dividend and modest upside, appealing to investors seeking income and stability. The article discusses how the company's performance is tied to interest rates, regulatory environments in high-growth states, and capital spending on grid modernization and renewables. While analysts see DUK as fairly valued, not cheap, its role as a core holding for yield and stability, particularly in income-oriented portfolios, remains intact despite ongoing regulatory risks and the complexities of the clean energy transition.

feed.title: Zacks.com featured highlights include DaVita, Ford, Centerra, Southwest Airlines and Patria Investments
feed.summary: This article identifies five value stocks—DaVita, Ford, Centerra Gold, Southwest Airlines, and Patria Investments Ltd.—to consider amidst Federal Reserve interest rate debates and market uncertainty. The selections are based on strong fundamentals, reasonable valuations, and high earnings yields, which are presented as a key indicator for undervalued stocks. The article highlights each company's strong projected earnings growth and Zacks Rank, recommending them as solid investment opportunities.

feed.title: Vornado Realty Trust Could Soar If These 2 Things Go Right
feed.summary: Vornado Realty Trust (NYSE: VNO) has seen its value drop 30% over the past year due to high interest rates and a challenging office market. However, the REIT could recover significantly if long-term interest rates decline and the office market continues its recovery. Recent data suggests positive trends in office leasing activity and sales volume, indicating a potential turnaround for Vornado.

feed.title: Barclays share price in focus: buyback update hits tape after ex-dividend slide
feed.summary: Barclays' share price fell by 3.7% after going ex-dividend and amidst ongoing buybacks, with the bank having repurchased 2,637,000 shares on Feb. 19. The market is weighing hopes for UK rate cuts against concerns over potential motor finance redress costs, with Barclays having provisioned £325 million for the latter. The bank's capital return program, totaling over 21 million shares bought back since Feb. 10, remains a key driver for investor confidence.

feed.title: Here's What the Street Thinks About Regions Financial Corporation (RF)
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan and UBS have raised their price targets for Regions Financial Corporation (RF), maintaining a Hold rating, amid expectations of elevated long-term interest rates and a favorable regulatory environment for banks. Despite missing Q4 2025 revenue and EPS estimates, RF's stock has seen over a 7% gain since its earnings release. The analysts anticipate the banking sector to remain stable due to sector rotation, economic trends, and strong fundamentals.

feed.title: Bond holders dubbed ‘losers’ in currency debasement crisis
feed.summary: Bitwise Asset Management warns that bondholders are poised to become "losers" in the face of ongoing currency debasement, record debt, and a weakening US dollar. The firm argues that traditional 60/40 portfolios are no longer effective and suggests allocating towards hard assets like gold and Bitcoin to preserve purchasing power. This shift is highlighted by the recent launch of the Bitwise Proficio Currency Debasement ETF (BPRO) to hedge against fiat currency devaluation.

feed.title: PCM Fund’s Double?Digit Yield: Opportunity or Value Trap for Income Investors?
feed.summary: PCM Fund Inc (NYSE: PCM), a PIMCO-managed closed-end fund, offers a high yield by investing in mortgage-related and income-producing securities with leverage. While attractive for income-focused investors, its substantial yield comes with risks such as sensitivity to interest rate changes, credit spread fluctuations, and liquidity concerns inherent in CEFs. Investors need to carefully assess the fund's discount/premium to NAV, distribution sustainability, and their own risk tolerance before considering it for their portfolios.

feed.title: Louisiana-Pacific Faces Rising Interest Rate Risk From Variable-Rate Amended Credit Facility
feed.summary: Louisiana-Pacific Corp (LPX) is facing increased interest rate risk due to its Amended Credit Facility, which uses variable benchmarks like base rate and Adjusted Term SOFR. This exposure means that rising market rates or credit spreads could significantly increase its interest expenses, negatively impacting cash flows, profitability, and covenant compliance. Analysts have set an average stock price target of $105.80, suggesting a 19.06% upside potential for LPX.

feed.title: Opendoor stock jumps 17% premarket after Q4 results — what traders watch next for OPEN
feed.summary: Opendoor (OPEN) stock surged 17% premarket after its Q4 earnings report, highlighting quicker home sales and reduced inventory. Despite a revenue decrease to $736 million and a net loss of $1.096 billion due to a debt charge, the company reported a 46% increase in homes bought quarter-on-quarter. Opendoor aims for breakeven adjusted net income by late 2026, but faces challenges from a volatile housing market, rising borrowing costs, and declining revenue expectations for Q1.

feed.title: Navigating Rate Hikes: The iShares Floating Rate Bond ETF's Upcoming Payout
feed.summary: The iShares Treasury Floating Rate Bond ETF (TFLO), with $6.45 billion in assets, offers investors a way to manage interest rate exposure by investing in U.S. government bonds with variable interest rates. Its unique structure, which links coupon payments to short-term U.S. Treasury bill auctions, allows its income to increase with rising benchmark rates, making it resilient to monetary policy shifts. The ETF has an ex-dividend date set for March 2, 2026, with payment on March 5, 2026, and undergoes monthly index rebalancing to maintain alignment with its benchmark.

feed.title: Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Fulton Financial Corporation
feed.summary: The Federal Reserve Board has approved the application by Fulton Financial Corporation of Lancaster, Pennsylvania, to acquire Blue Foundry Bancorp and its subsidiary, Blue Foundry Bank, both located in Rutherford, New Jersey. This announcement was made for release at 4:30 p.m. EST on Thursday, February 19, 2026.

feed.title: Harmony Gold Stock: Quiet Rally, Big Fed-and-Gold Upside?
feed.summary: Harmony Gold Mining Company Ltd (HGMCY) is experiencing a quiet rally driven by surging gold prices, yet it remains undervalued compared to its peers. The article suggests that Harmony offers leveraged gold exposure for US investors, especially given expectations about the Federal Reserve's cautious interest rate cuts and elevated geopolitical tensions. Despite its potential, it advises investors to consider the accompanying above-average political and operational risks associated with its South African and Papua New Guinea mining assets.

feed.title: Harmony Gold Stock: Quiet Rally, Big Fed-and-Gold Upside?
feed.summary: Harmony Gold Mining Company Ltd (Harmony) is exhibiting strong performance in line with rising gold prices, yet its stock trades at a discount compared to global peers. This presents a unique opportunity for US investors seeking leveraged gold exposure. The article explores how improving balance sheet metrics and operational delivery, alongside gold's resilience in a "higher-for-longer" Fed scenario, are reshaping Harmony's risk/reward profile.

feed.title: Galaxy Digital CEO Calls Bitcoin a 'Generational Asset' Despite Bear Market Pressures
feed.summary: Michael Novogratz, CEO of Galaxy Digital, reiterated his belief that Bitcoin is a long-term, generational asset, despite the current crypto bear market. He emphasized Bitcoin's unique value proposition and potential to benefit from macroeconomic trends and wider adoption. Novogratz's comments highlight how a major crypto firm views the market downturn and Bitcoin's future amid increasing competition from other speculative assets.

feed.title: IBD Rating Upgrades: Oklo Shows Improved Relative Price Strength
feed.summary: Oklo (OKLO) has seen its Relative Strength (RS) Rating increase from 68 to 73, indicating improved market leadership. This proprietary rating tracks a stock's 52-week price movement against other stocks. The article highlights Oklo's recent upgrades and mentions related news discussing its rising market leadership and other financial activities.

feed.title: ETF issuers devise new ways to slice Europe
feed.summary: In 2025, renewed investor interest in European equities led ETF issuers like DWS, Amundi, First Trust, and HANetf to launch new products to capture this market. These new ETFs employ either multi-theme or multi-factor strategies to offer diverse exposure to the continent's recovery and growth. This article details the different approaches taken by these issuers, including thematic ETFs focusing on European self-reliance and factor-based ETFs identifying market leaders and growth strength.

feed.title: The Top 5 Analyst Questions From Advance Auto Parts's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, beating expectations on profitability and earnings despite a slight revenue decline. Management highlighted foundational changes in operations and store closures as keys to margin expansion, while analysts focused on SKU inflation, real estate strategy, and the launch of the new ARGOS private label.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse continues to navigate high beef costs
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse successfully navigated high beef costs in 2025 by increasing menu prices and focusing on consistent growth, celebrating its 33rd anniversary with positive sales and traffic. The company saw a 4.2% increase in fourth-quarter same-store sales and 1.9% traffic growth, despite a decrease in restaurant-level margins due to commodity and labor inflation. Texas Roadhouse is also investing in beverages like mocktails and digital kitchen technology to further enhance efficiency and customer experience.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Stock After Earnings: Undervalued Bank or Value Trap?
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp (USB) recently released earnings, showing it is navigating deposit pricing pressure and cooling rate-cut hopes. The company's net interest income has plateaued, but fee-based businesses are providing a buffer, and credit quality, while normalizing, remains manageable. Despite potential regulatory capital increases, USB's dividend appears secure, positioning it as a quality, income-oriented regional bank with moderate upside for long-term investors.

feed.title: Regional Banks Stocks Q4 In Review: BancFirst (NASDAQ:BANF) Vs Peers
feed.summary: BancFirst (NASDAQ:BANF) reported mixed Q4 2025 results with a revenue beat but an EPS miss, causing its stock to drop. Other regional banks experienced varied outcomes, with Merchants Bancorp (NASDAQ:MBIN) showing strong performance and The Bancorp (NASDAQ:TBBK) delivering a disappointing quarter. The article also provides a brief market update, noting the Fed's interest rate policies and the impact of the 2024 presidential election on the stock market.

feed.title: Gold Royalty upsizes credit facility to $150M, cuts interest rate
feed.summary: Gold Royalty Corp. has increased its revolving credit facility to $150 million from $90 million and concurrently reduced the interest rate. This financial move provides the company with enhanced flexibility and liquidity, signaling potential for future growth and operations.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison misses quarterly profit estimates on higher operating and interest expenses
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED.N) reported fourth-quarter profit that missed Wall Street estimates due to higher operating and interest expenses. The utility's operating expenses rose to $3.51 billion and interest expenses increased to $313 million. The company forecasts full-year adjusted profit in the range of $6 to $6.20 per share and expects capital investments of approximately $6.56 billion in 2026 and $6.76 billion in 2027.

feed.title: Study: Risk-Managed ETFs Didn't Meet 2022 Challenges
feed.summary: A recent study found that over half of risk-managed ETFs designed to mitigate losses performed worse than equities in 2022, including some marketed for black swan events. While some strategies like ASYMmetric Smart S&P 500 ETF (ASPY) and Innovator U.S. Equity Buffer ETF (BJAN) managed to meet downside protection goals over a two-year period, others failed, often due to an over-reliance on long-term Treasurys which suffered from rising interest rates. Experts emphasize the need for dynamic approaches to risk management, considering various economic factors beyond just volatility.

feed.title: EQUIFAX INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Equifax Inc. has released its 2025 10-K report, highlighting significant financial growth with operating revenue of $6,074.5 million, a 7% increase from 2024, and net income up 9% to $664.3 million. The report details strategic initiatives including continued investments in technology, product innovation, and capital management activities like share repurchases and dividend increases, while also outlining challenges such as market competition, technology risks, regulatory changes, and economic conditions.

feed.title: Why Pool (POOL) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: Shares of Pool Corporation (NASDAQ:POOL) fell 13% after the company reported fourth-quarter 2025 earnings and revenue that missed analyst expectations and provided a weaker-than-anticipated forecast for 2026. Net sales were flat year-on-year, and earnings per share declined, indicating concerns about future profitability and modest growth. The stock is trading significantly below its 52-week high, with a $1,000 investment made five years ago now worth $679.30.

feed.title: Bancolombia Stock Jumps on Colombia Rate Cut Hopes: Buy the Dip?
feed.summary: Bancolombia's US-listed shares are outperforming many US bank stocks due to Colombia's aggressive interest-rate cutting cycle, making it an appealing option for investors seeking emerging-market financial exposure. The article explores the bank's risk/reward profile, influenced by macro conditions, FX rates, and political considerations, while also providing guidance for US investors on how to incorporate Bancolombia into their portfolios. Despite the rally, Bancolombia trades at a discount to global peers, but investors should monitor central bank decisions, earnings, and political developments.

feed.title: Key facts: Altria Group shifts to smoke-free products; strong Marlboro sales
feed.summary: Altria Group is focusing on transitioning to smoke-free products, having observed significant growth in its oral tobacco and nicotine pouch segments. Despite inflation, the company also reported strong Marlboro sales, highlighting the resilience of its premium cigarette brands.

feed.title: Vornado Realty Trust Could Soar If These 2 Things Go Right
feed.summary: Vornado Realty Trust has seen its stock price drop due to high interest rates and a challenging office market. However, a significant decline in long-term interest rates and continued recovery in the office market could turn these headwinds into tailwinds, potentially leading to a strong recovery for the REIT. The article highlights that the office market is showing signs of recovery with increased leasing activity and investor confidence, while decreasing long-term interest rates would lower borrowing costs and increase real estate values for Vornado.

feed.title: REITs Set for a 2026 Rebound? 7 Top Picks as Rate Cuts Approach
feed.summary: Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs), which underperformed in 2024 and 2025 due to rising interest rates, are poised for a significant rebound in 2026 as rate cuts approach. Brad Thomas of Wide Moat Research recommends seven REITs, including stable operators like Realty Income, Equinix, and Public Storage, and higher-risk, high-potential options such as Americold Realty Trust and Healthpeak Properties with specific catalysts for growth. This shift presents an opportunity for investors to position themselves early before valuations increase.

feed.title: Valley Bank survey: Businesses start 2026 with momentum, but face external, operating pressures
feed.summary: A Valley National Bank survey indicates that middle-market commercial and industrial businesses performed strongly in 2025. However, these businesses are entering 2026 facing rising external and operating pressures, including inflation, interest rates, and geopolitical tensions. Despite this, businesses remain optimistic and are prioritizing financial and operational efficiency, customer retention, pricing strategy, AI adoption, data analytics, and cost efficiency for the new year.

feed.title: TransUnion: Purchase Mortgages To Lead Credit Expansion
feed.summary: TransUnion forecasts that mortgage originations will continue to rise through 2026, primarily driven by purchase mortgages. This growth is attributed to easing interest rates, improved affordability, and strong consumer demand, alongside a gradual recovery in refinance activity. While overall lending expansion is expected to moderate compared to 2025, mortgages and unsecured personal loans are projected to be key drivers of credit growth, though rising mortgage delinquencies highlight a need for disciplined underwriting.

feed.title: Liberty Wealth Buys $19 Million of Dimensional Global Ex US Core Fixed Income ETF
feed.summary: Liberty Wealth Management LLC recently acquired 367,041 shares of Dimensional Global Ex US Core Fixed Income ETF (DFGX) for an estimated value of $19.3 million, marking a new position that represents 2.5% of its U.S. equity assets under management. This investment highlights Liberty Wealth's balanced strategy, incorporating bond funds like DFGX for diversification and income, alongside growth-oriented assets. The move is strategically timed, with investors anticipating further interest rate cuts in 2026, making bond funds a way to secure higher yields.

feed.title: Global Economic Outlook: February 2026
feed.summary: This February 2026 report from S&P Global analyzes the global economic outlook, highlighting the impact of geopolitical uncertainty on markets despite resilient economic conditions suggested by PMI data. It details key changes in their forecast, including higher real GDP growth projections for the US, India, and the eurozone, leading to an increased global growth forecast of 2.9% for 2026. The report also addresses consumer price inflation expectations, noting projected moderations driven by oil prices and easing underlying pressures, and discusses factors influencing US dollar depreciation and Federal Reserve policy.

feed.title: P&G at the 2026 Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference
feed.summary: P&G executives discussed adaptations to its integrated growth strategy at the 2026 CAGNY Conference, addressing external changes like media fragmentation, inflation, and retail evolution. The company plans near-term interventions focusing on consumer-centricity, transforming brand building, and holistic retailer partnerships. P&G aims to leverage its strengths to proactively shape the future of the CPG industry.
